title: The Political Prize for Morocco’s Winning Football Team
summary: Fouzi Lekjaa is widely credited with turning around Morocco’s football fortunes.

title: Tesla Holders Bet on SpaceX Merger as Musk’s Real Endgame
summary: It’s been a challenging year for Tesla Inc. shareholders, losing 15% while watching Elon Musk’s SpaceX complete the biggest initial public offering ever and immediately become the most valuable entity in the newly minted trillionaire’s collection.

title: Gold Slumps as Tech Selloff Reverberates Through Global Markets
summary: Gold declined toward $4,000 an ounce and silver plunged as much as 5.8% as a broad selloff swept through financial markets.

title: Stocks Slide as Wall Street Gets AI Wake-Up Call: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street got a reality check as a bruising selloff in several technology giants fueled concern the artificial-intelligence frenzy that has powered the equity bull market might be overblown.

title: Sony Returns to US Bond Market After Nearly Three Decades
summary: Sony Group Corp. was still marketing the original PlayStation when it last tapped the US investment-grade bond market. Almost three decades later, it’s made a comeback.

title: Yen Intervention Prospect Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
summary: Currency traders are on high alert for intervention after further weakness in the yen and a call between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

title: SpaceX Set to Raise $25 Billion of Bonds in Debut Offer
summary: Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI conglomerate is set to raise $25 billion of bonds in five parts on Tuesday, in its debut bond offer, and is paying a relatively wide premium over Treasuries to get the deal done, people familiar with the matter said. Ed Ludlow has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ether-Linked Nonprofit Cuts Budget 40% In ‘Challenging Era’
summary: Vitalik Buterin, the programmer who helped create underlying technology for Ether, the world’s No. 2 cryptocurrency, said the nonprofit behind it is being forced to make some difficult decisions.

title: Oil Eases While Tankers Openly Enter Hormuz After Peace Deal
summary: Oil dipped as tankers become more overt in transiting the Strait of Hormuz, a sign that a much-anticipated rush of barrels is en route to quell one of the most severe supply shocks in history.

title: Macklem Says Bank Capital Rule Change Alone Won’t Spark Lending
summary: Looser bank capital rules alone won’t boost economic activity, according to Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem, echoing analysts who questioned whether a recent regulatory change would immediately spur more lending without borrowers at the door.

title: Argentine Economy Grew More Than Expected in First Quarter
summary: Economic growth in Argentina proved resilient in the first quarter, surpassing expectations even as unemployment rose and wages trailed inflation.

title: Cboe Revives Binary Options to Compete with Kalshi, Polymarket
summary: Cboe Global Markets, Inc. announced Tuesday that it has brought back a type of S&P 500 contract after a hiatus of more than a decade, which in the meantime has become a popular mainstay of prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket.

title: JPMorgan’s New Credit Funds Win SEC Nod for Monthly Redemptions
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. won approval to offer monthly redemptions on a new interval fund investing in private and public credit, as the direct-lending industry grapples with another round of elevated requests to pull out cash.

title: We Can't Use The Strategic Petroleum Reserve For Political Purposes Says Mike Sommers
summary: Mike Sommers, president of the American Petroleum Institute, joined Balance of Power to discuss the American Energy Security Framework. Sommers said the nation's Strategic Petroleum Reserve is "in desperate need of upgrading." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Natural Gas Drops on Cooler Outlooks, New Pipeline Capacity
summary: US natural gas futures declined as forecasts shifted to show cooler weather in the coming weeks, suggesting a decline in demand for gas-fired electricity as consumers pull back on using air-conditioners. Kinder Morgan’s Gulf Coast Express pipeline expansion project also entered service, indicating more gas will be moving out of the oversupplied Permian Basin into surrounding regions instead of being flared.

title: Trump Call for Crop Sales to Iran Adds Cautious Hope for Farmers
summary: President Donald Trump’s call for Iran to buy US crops as part of a peace deal is being met with a mix of doubt and cautious optimism by American farmers.

title: Trump Says Iran’s Unfrozen Funds to Remain Under US Control
summary: President Donald Trump said Iran will only be able to use funds released from frozen accounts to purchase food and medical supplies from the US, seeking to ease concerns about peace negotiations that both sides say are making progress.  Brett Bruen, President of the Global Situation Room and former White House Director of Global Engagement joined Balance of Power to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasuries See Good Auction Demand as Rate-Hike Bets Trimmed
summary: Treasuries rose as a selloff in US stocks and further declines in oil prices curbed wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate increases over the coming year.

title: Bristow to Acquire Berry Aviation in Government Push
summary: Bristow Group Inc. agreed to buy closely held Berry Aviation Inc. as it seeks to expand in the government services sector.

title: A Jevons Paradox in Bureaucracy
summary: Will AI boost productivity?

title: Heineken Names Coffee CEO Oliveira to Lead Turnaround
summary: Heineken NV appointed Rafael Oliveira chief executive officer, as the Dutch brewer breaks with habit by hiring an outsider to try to reverse a slump in demand.

title: Korea AI Rout Revives Scrutiny of $290 Billion Levered-ETF Boom
summary: South Korea’s AI-fueled selloff has put a spotlight back on one of the fastest-growing corners of retail investing: leveraged exchange-traded funds.

title: Schick Razor Maker Edgewell’s Shares Leap on Rejected Offer
summary: Shares of Edgewell Personal Care Co., which makes Schick razors, rose nearly 18% in New York on Tuesday after the company rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from private equity firm Yellow Wood Partners.

title: Dollar Jumps to Highest Since November on Fed Rate Hike Bets
summary: The dollar touched the highest level since November on Tuesday as traders cement views on the Federal Reserve hiking interest rates this year.

title: Sharif Assures Pezeshkian Pakistan Won’t Let Down Iran
summary: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif assured the visiting Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian his country won’t let down Iran, as the two leaders met in Islamabad after the US-Iran truce that Pakistan helped mediate.

title: How Eli Lilly’s CEO Plans to Defeat Pharma’s Boom-Bust Cycle
summary:
When Dave Ricks became chief executive officer of Eli Lilly & Co. in 2017, he was facing an industry under siege. 

Americans held drug companies in lower esteem than airlines, law firms and even the federal government. President Donald Trump, then starting his first term, was excoriating them for their high prices. A mounting number of reports showed that soaring costs were even forcing some diabetics to ration insulin. Ricks, the 11th CEO in Lilly’s 150-year history, concedes that one was a public-relations nightmare.

Almost a decade later, the mood at Lilly has shifted dramatically. The company’s hit diabetes shot, Mounjaro, and obesity shot, Zepbound, have transformed its fortunes and, in many ways, its public image. We speak with Madison Muller, Bloomberg News Healthcare Reporter, for a closer look. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Maritime Watchdog to Start Evacuating Stranded Seafarers From Middle East
summary: The International Maritime Organization said it has received safety guarantees that could allow hundreds of ships to exit the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz, as it seeks to evacuate thousands of seafarers.

title: Gas-Station Giant EG Group Confidentially Files for $1 Billion US IPO
summary: EG Group, the gas station and convenience store operator, has confidentially filed for a US initial public offering that could raise about $1 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Strict Rent Control Proposal Is Struck Down in Massachusetts
summary: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court struck down a ballot proposal that would have imposed the strictest statewide rent control in the US.

title: Tech Selloff Hits Markets | Open Interest 6/23/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Global markets get a reality check as tech stocks slide again, while SpaceX fights to defend its $2 trillion valuation after three straight days of losses. Currency traders brace for potential yen intervention. Plus, Apollo caps withdrawals from a major private credit fund as redemption pressures build. And America's nuclear renaissance accelerates. Oklo Co-Founder and CEO Jake DeWitte joins Bloomberg Open Interest on the race to power the AI boom with next-generation reactors. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pemex and Petrobras to Team Up on Oil and Refining Projects
summary: The national oil companies of Mexico and Brazil agreed to work together to discover, produce and refine oil as both Latin American energy giants push to expand their reserves.
